description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk with a square base and a tapering, four-sided shaft that culminates in a pointed tip. The base is broad and features a raised plinth with a recessed panel on each face, some of which appear to have worn engravings or reliefs. The shaft above the base is slightly narrower and rises to a pyramidal cap, which is adorned with a circular medallion or emblem on each side. The surface of the obelisk is mottled with greenish and grayish hues, suggesting age and exposure to the elements, possibly moss or patina. The overall design is simple yet dignified, with minimal ornamentation aside from the medallions and faint reliefs. This type of monument is commonly found in parks, plazas, or as a commemorative marker in urban environments.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a square base, recessed panels, and a pyramidal cap featuring circular medallions. The surface is mottled gray-green, showing signs of age and exposure.
description_view_0: Front view showing a recessed panel on the base and a circular medallion on the cap, with the surface appearing relatively clean and defined.
description_view_1: Side view with similar features as the front, but the panel appears less defined and the medallion is visible at a slight angle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a more eroded or worn panel on the base and a medallion on the cap, with more visible surface weathering.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a heavily weathered panel and medallion, showing the most pronounced surface discoloration and wear.
attribute_inscription: None visible; panels appear to have worn engravings or reliefs, but no legible text or clear symbols can be identified.
attribute_ornamentation: Circular medallions or emblems are present on each face of the pyramidal cap; faint reliefs or engravings are visible on the recessed panels of the base, but details are indistinct due to weathering.
attribute_base_design: The base is square and robust, featuring a raised plinth with a recessed rectangular panel on each face. The base sits on a slightly wider, rough-edged platform, giving it a solid and grounded appearance.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is a simple pyramidal cap, slightly rounded at the apex, with a circular medallion or emblem on each face. The finish is rough and weathered, with no additional decorative features.
